title: Utilities: 1998 Industry Practice Summary
author: Aberdeen Group
abstract: Aberdeen Group's 1998 practice summary covering information technology for electric and natural gas utilities facing deregulation. The document defines three practice pillars — Distribution (billing/CIS/call centers/meter networks), Asset Management (SCADA/EMS/ERP/GIS), and Transactions (energy trading/risk management/ISO systems) — and profiles 12 vendors. Key findings include that U.S. gas and electric utilities represent $300B in revenues; the last major U.S. monopoly being deregulated; and that conservative IT buying behavior will coexist with urgent spending on billing, CIS, and customer-facing systems.
importance_rationale: Captures IT landscape at the onset of U.S. utility deregulation, a once-in-a-generation structural shift; profiles specialized vendors rarely covered in general IT research.
relevance_rationale: Utility deregulation dynamics, SCADA modernization, smart metering, and the CIS/ERP integration challenge all remain active IT investment areas in the 2020s.
